author: e-ddykim
title: Smart CI fails: fastcore 2.0.1 breaks ghapi 1.0.4 - L.starmap AttributeError

failed_jobs.name: Smart_CI
failed_jobs.symptom: AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'starmap' when constructing GhApi()

failed_jobs.name: ci/gha_overall_status_ubuntu_22_arm64_cross_compilation
failed_jobs.symptom: Downstream status check exits 1 due to Smart_CI failure

key_errors:
File "/home/runner/.local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/ghapi/core.py", line 105, in __init__
    funcs_ = L(funcs).starmap(_GhVerb, client=self, kwargs=kwargs)
             ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
File "/home/runner/.local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/fastcore/basics.py", line 578, in __getattr__
    if attr is not None: return getattr(attr,k)
                               ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'starmap'
root_cause: The smart-ci requirements.txt pins ghapi==1.0.4 but specifies fastcore>=1.5.4 with no upper bound. fastcore 2.0.1 was resolved and installed, but it is incompatible with ghapi 1.0.4: the L class no longer provides a 'starmap' method (or returns a plain list) that ghapi 1.0.4 depends on in GhApi.__init__ (core.py line 105). This is the second occurrence of this failure (same issue seen on run 29000379233 affecting PR #36780 ~15 minutes earlier). Fix: pin fastcore to fastcore>=1.5.4,<2.0.0 in .github/actions/smart-ci/requirements.txt.
reproduction_steps: pip install ghapi==1.0.4 fastcore==2.0.1; python3 -c "from ghapi.all import GhApi; GhApi()"  # This reproduces the AttributeError
recommended_actions: Pin fastcore in .github/actions/smart-ci/requirements.txt to fastcore>=1.5.4,<2.0.0 to prevent future breaks from fastcore major version bumps.
Alternatively, upgrade ghapi to a version that supports fastcore 2.x.
After fixing, re-run the failed jobs to restore the merge queue.
prevention_strategies: Always specify upper bounds for transitive dependencies in requirements.txt files used by CI infrastructure scripts. Consider using pip-compile or a lockfile. Add a CI sanity-check step that imports the smart-ci module before running it.
historical_context: This is the second occurrence of this specific failure on 2026-07-09. First occurrence: run 29000379233 (PR #36780, Linux RISC-V Ubuntu 22.04, ~07:11Z). The fastcore 2.0.1 release appears to have broken the ghapi 1.0.4 API for all merge-queue CI workflows today.
